creator: rbmills@lucent.com
text:
Sheldon,

Sorry about the "it didn't work" problem description. I should have
known better, having received this sort of problem report myself
may times.

It turns out I have made a fool of myself. You are right, I recreated
the boot floppy and all is OK.

What had me believing there was a problem is that the kern.flp file
in the cdrom is different from the one downloaded from:

 ftp://ftp.FreeBSD.org/pub/FreeBSD/releases/i386/4.1-RELEASE/floppies/kern.flp

I appologise for causing any inconvenience. I'll ensure that I retest
any suspect problems before reporting them next time. You guys are doing
a great job and it certainly was not my intention to throw bogus
bug reports at you.


Thanks for the prompt response,


Robert Mills


On Mon, 7 Aug 2000, Sheldon Hearn wrote:

> 
> 
> On Sun, 06 Aug 2000 21:05:01 MST, rbmills@lucent.com wrote:
> 
> > I created "kern" and "mfsroot" boot floppies using the images
> > contained on the cdrom image downloaded from:
> > 
> >   ftp://ftp.FreeBSD.org/pub/FreeBSD/releases/i386/ISO-IMAGES/4.1-install.iso
> > 
> > These floppies would not work, so I download new copies from:
> 
> "These floppies would not work" doesn't help much.  Could you explain
> exactly what you saw?
> 
> Furthermore, are you convinced that you haven't just been bitten by bad
> floppies, or badly written floppies?  You wouldn't be the first person
> to have tripped up over this. :-)
> 
> Ciao,
> Sheldon.
>
